task_completion.description: The rocket's stability margin is increased to at least 1.5 calibers by enlarging the fins.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the stability margin indicator in the OpenRocket design window (usually top right) showing >= 1.5 cal, or the Flight Simulations tab showing stability >= 1.5. The agent should be seen editing the fin dimensions to be larger than the initial 15mm.

task_completion.description: The parachute sizes are increased to achieve a safe ground hit velocity of <= 6 m/s.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Flight Simulations tab showing a 'Ground hit velocity' of 6 m/s or less. The agent should be seen editing the parachute diameters (e.g., main >= 400mm, drogue >= 150mm).

task_completion.description: A motor is selected and the rocket is tuned to achieve the target apogee and acceleration.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Flight Simulations tab showing an 'Apogee' between 400 and 500 m, and a 'Max. acceleration' of 100 m/s² or less.

task_completion.description: The final rocket design is saved to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the OpenRocket 'Save As' dialog targeting '/home/ga/Documents/rockets/competition_final.ork', or the main window title changing to 'competition_final.ork'.

task_completion.description: The flight simulation data is exported to a CSV file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the OpenRocket 'Export data' dialog targeting '/home/ga/Documents/exports/flight_data.csv'.

task_completion.description: A design optimization report is written containing the required metrics.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor (like gedit or nano) or terminal showing the contents of '/home/ga/Documents/exports/design_report.txt'. The text must include the motor choice, stability margin, predicted apogee, maximum acceleration, and descent performance.

integrity.description: The agent used the OpenRocket GUI to modify the rocket and run simulations.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for interaction with the OpenRocket application windows, rather than just writing a script to generate the .ork or .csv files.

integrity.description: The metrics written in the design report match the simulation results.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the values for apogee, acceleration, stability, and descent in the text report match the numbers seen in the OpenRocket Flight Simulations tab.

integrity.description: The flight data CSV was exported from OpenRocket, not manually fabricated.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the agent using the 'Export Data' feature in OpenRocket rather than manually creating or echoing text into a CSV file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The starting rocket has undersized fins (15mm), main parachute (150mm), and drogue (50mm). The agent must enlarge fins to >=50mm to achieve >=1.5 cal stability. Parachutes must be enlarged (main >=400mm, drogue >=150mm) to achieve <=6 m/s ground hit velocity. The agent must select a motor (e.g., AeroTech G64W or similar) and tune the mass (e.g., via ballast) to achieve 400-500m apogee and <=100 m/s² max acceleration. Output files must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/rockets/competition_final.ork, /home/ga/Documents/exports/flight_data.csv, and /home/ga/Documents/exports/design_report.txt.
